ucs.title: [LLM] APT34 (OilRig) DNS-tunnel C2 — Helminth/DNSpionage TXT-record beaconing
ucs.description: Hunts the high-cadence outbound DNS traffic that defines OilRig's signature backdoors (Helminth, DNSpionage, ALMA Communicator, Karkoff, SideTwist) which encode C2 in TXT/A queries to actor-controlled domains. Looks for a single endpoint generating an unusually high volume of distinct subdomains under a single parent domain — the fingerprint of base-encoded data exfiltrated label-by-label.
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=t count dc(Network_Traffic.dest) as uniq_subdomains values(Network_Traffic.dest) as sample_subs from datamodel=Network_Traffic.All_Traffic where Network_Traffic.dest_port=53 Network_Traffic.app=dns by Network_Traffic.src _time span=10m Network_Traffic.dest | rename Network_Traffic.* as * | eval parent_domain=mvindex(split(dest,"."),-2)+"."+mvindex(split(dest,"."),-1) | stats count as queries dc(dest) as unique_fqdns values(dest) as sampled by src parent_domain _time | where unique_fqdns >= 50 AND queries >= 100 | where NOT match(parent_domain,"(microsoft|windows|google|apple|amazonaws|akamai|office|live|msn|cloudfront|azureedge|trafficmanager)\.(com|net)$")
ucs.kql:
// APT34/OilRig DNS-tunnel C2 — Helminth/DNSpionage style TXT beaconing
let LookbackHours = 24h;
let KnownBenignParents = dynamic(["microsoft.com","windows.com","office.com","office365.com","live.com","msn.com","google.com","gstatic.com","apple.com","icloud.com","amazonaws.com","cloudfront.net","akamai.net","akamaiedge.net","azureedge.net","trafficmanager.net","in-addr.arpa"]);
DeviceEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(LookbackHours)
| where ActionType == "DnsQueryResponse"
| extend Q = tolower(tostring(parse_json(AdditionalFields).QueryName))
| where isnotempty(Q)
| extend Labels = split(Q, ".")
| extend ParentDomain = strcat(tostring(Labels[array_length(Labels)-2]), ".", tostring(Labels[array_length(Labels)-1]))
| extend Subdomain = iff(array_length(Labels) > 2, strcat_array(array_slice(Labels, 0, array_length(Labels)-3), "."), "")
| where isnotempty(Subdomain)
| where ParentDomain !in (KnownBenignParents)
| where strlen(Subdomain) >= 12        // OilRig encodes data in subdomain — short ones are usually legit CDN sharding
| summarize TotalQueries = count(),
            UniqueSubdomains = dcount(Subdomain),
            FirstSeen = min(Timestamp),
            LastSeen = max(Timestamp),
            SampleQueries = make_set(Q, 10),
            Processes = make_set(InitiatingProcessFileName, 10)
            by DeviceId, DeviceName, ParentDomain, bin(Timestamp, 1h)
| where UniqueSubdomains >= 50         // 50 distinct subdomains/hr to one parent = beaconing, not browsing
  and TotalQueries >= 100              // sustained, not bursty
| extend BeaconRatio = round(todouble(UniqueSubdomains) / todouble(TotalQueries), 2)   // ~1.0 means every query is a unique label = exfil
| where BeaconRatio > 0.5
| order by UniqueSubdomains desc
ucs.sentinel_kql:
// APT34/OilRig DNS-tunnel C2 — Helminth/DNSpionage style TXT beaconing on Windows DNS server logs
let LookbackHours = 24h;
let KnownBenignParents = dynamic(["microsoft.com","windows.com","office.com","office365.com","live.com","msn.com","google.com","gstatic.com","apple.com","icloud.com","amazonaws.com","cloudfront.net","akamai.net","akamaiedge.net","azureedge.net","trafficmanager.net","in-addr.arpa"]);
DnsEvents
| where TimeGenerated > ago(LookbackHours)
| where QueryType in ("A","AAAA","TXT")
| extend Q = tolower(Name)
| where isnotempty(Q)
| extend Labels = split(Q, ".")
| extend ParentDomain = strcat(tostring(Labels[array_length(Labels)-2]), ".", tostring(Labels[array_length(Labels)-1]))
| extend Subdomain = iff(array_length(Labels) > 2, strcat_array(array_slice(Labels, 0, array_length(Labels)-3), "."), "")
| where isnotempty(Subdomain)
| where ParentDomain !in (KnownBenignParents)
| where strlen(Subdomain) >= 12
| summarize TotalQueries = count(),
            UniqueSubdomains = dcount(Subdomain),
            TxtRatio = countif(QueryType == "TXT") * 1.0 / count(),
            FirstSeen = min(TimeGenerated),
            LastSeen = max(TimeGenerated),
            SampleQueries = make_set(Name, 10)
            by ClientIP, ParentDomain, bin(TimeGenerated, 1h)
| where UniqueSubdomains >= 50 and TotalQueries >= 100
| extend BeaconRatio = round(todouble(UniqueSubdomains) / todouble(TotalQueries), 2)
| where BeaconRatio > 0.5
| order by UniqueSubdomains desc
ucs.rationale: DNS-tunnel C2 over TXT/A records is the hallmark technique of every documented OilRig backdoor since 2016 — Helminth, DNSpionage, ALMA Communicator, Karkoff, SideTwist, OilCheck, Saitama. Iran-attributed groups in this cluster prefer DNS because Iranian-targeted Middle-East government / energy networks frequently allow port-53 egress to ANY domain even when 80/443 is filtered. The detection's sign

ucs.title: [LLM] APT34 (OilRig) IIS web shell — TwoFace/RGDoor: w3wp.exe spawning cmd/powershell on Exchange/OWA
ucs.description: Catches OilRig's signature IIS-server foothold (TwoFace ASPX dropper, RGDoor IIS native module) by detecting w3wp.exe — the IIS worker process — spawning interactive command-line interpreters. This pattern is virtually impossible in benign IIS workloads and is the post-exploitation step Mandiant/Microsoft repeatedly attribute to Helix Kitten / Hazel Sandstorm intrusions of Middle-East government Exchange and Outlook Web Access servers.
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=t count min(_time) as firstSeen max(_time) as lastSeen values(Processes.process) as cmdline values(Processes.user) as user values(Processes.process_hash) as child_hash from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where Processes.parent_process_name=w3wp.exe (Processes.process_name IN (cmd.exe,powershell.exe,pwsh.exe,powershell_ise.exe,cscript.exe,wscript.exe,mshta.exe,rundll32.exe,regsvr32.exe,certutil.exe,bitsadmin.exe,net.exe,net1.exe,whoami.exe,systeminfo.exe,nltest.exe,tasklist.exe,quser.exe,ipconfig.exe,arp.exe,nbtstat.exe)) by host Processes.parent_process Processes.process_name Processes.process | rename Processes.* as * | where NOT match(parent_process,"(?i)(MSExchangeHM|HealthMailbox|appcmd)")
ucs.kql:
// APT34/OilRig — TwoFace / RGDoor IIS web-shell post-exploitation
// w3wp.exe (IIS worker) spawning interactive shells, recon LOLBins, or download tools
let LookbackDays = 7d;
let ChildShells = dynamic(["cmd.exe","powershell.exe","pwsh.exe","powershell_ise.exe","cscript.exe","wscript.exe","mshta.exe","rundll32.exe","regsvr32.exe"]);
let ChildRecon  = dynamic(["net.exe","net1.exe","whoami.exe","systeminfo.exe","nltest.exe","tasklist.exe","quser.exe","ipconfig.exe","arp.exe","nbtstat.exe","hostname.exe","query.exe"]);
let ChildDownloaders = dynamic(["certutil.exe","bitsadmin.exe","curl.exe","wget.exe","ftp.exe"]);
DeviceProcessEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(LookbackDays)
| where InitiatingProcessFileName =~ "w3wp.exe"
| where FileName in~ (ChildShells) or FileName in~ (ChildRecon) or FileName in~ (ChildDownloaders)
| extend Bucket = case(
    FileName in~ (ChildShells), "Shell",
    FileName in~ (ChildRecon), "Recon",
    "Downloader")
| extend AppPool = extract(@"-ap\s+\"?([^\s\"]+)", 1, InitiatingProcessCommandLine)
| project Timestamp, DeviceName, AccountName, AccountDomain,
          AppPool, Bucket,
          ChildImage = FolderPath,
          ChildCmd = ProcessCommandLine,
          ChildSHA256 = SHA256,
          ParentImage = InitiatingProcessFolderPath,
          ParentCmd = InitiatingProcessCommandLine,
          IntegrityLevel = ProcessIntegrityLevel,
          IsRemote = IsInitiatingProcessRemoteSession
| order by Timestamp desc
ucs.sentinel_kql:
// APT34/OilRig — TwoFace / RGDoor IIS web-shell post-exploitation (Sentinel via 4688)
let LookbackDays = 7d;
let ChildShells = dynamic(["cmd.exe","powershell.exe","pwsh.exe","powershell_ise.exe","cscript.exe","wscript.exe","mshta.exe","rundll32.exe","regsvr32.exe"]);
let ChildRecon  = dynamic(["net.exe","net1.exe","whoami.exe","systeminfo.exe","nltest.exe","tasklist.exe","quser.exe","ipconfig.exe","arp.exe","nbtstat.exe","hostname.exe","query.exe"]);
let ChildDownloaders = dynamic(["certutil.exe","bitsadmin.exe","curl.exe","wget.exe","ftp.exe"]);
SecurityEvent
| where TimeGenerated > ago(LookbackDays)
| where EventID == 4688
| where ParentProcessName endswith "\\w3wp.exe"
| extend ChildName = tolower(tostring(split(NewProcessName, "\\")[-1]))
| where ChildName in (ChildShells) or ChildName in (ChildRecon) or ChildName in (ChildDownloaders)
| extend Bucket = case(
    ChildName in (ChildShells), "Shell",
    ChildName in (ChildRecon), "Recon",
    "Downloader")
| extend AppPool = extract(@"-ap\s+\"?([^\s\"]+)", 1, tostring(ParentProcessName))
| project TimeGenerated, Computer, Account, SubjectUserName,
          AppPool, Bucket,
          ChildImage = NewProcessName,
          ChildCmd = CommandLine,
          ParentImage = ParentProcessName,
          TokenElevationType
| order by TimeGenerated desc
ucs.sigma_yaml:
title: APT34 OilRig TwoFace/RGDoor IIS Web Shell - w3wp Spawning Shell or Recon
id: c1b2a3d4-9f8e-4c7a-b6d5-7e8f9a0b1c2d
status: experimental
description: Detects IIS worker process (w3wp.exe) spawning interactive shells, recon LOLBins, or downloaders - the post-exploitation pattern of APT34's TwoFace and RGDoor web shells used against Exchange/OWA servers.
references:
  - https://attack.mitre.org/groups/G0049/
  - https://www.mandiant.com/resources/blog/apt34-oilrig-twoface-webshell
author: SOC Detection Engineering
date: 2026/05/06
tags:
  - attack.persistence
  - attack.t1505.003
  - attack.execution
  - attack.t1059.001
  - attack.t1059.003
  - apt34
  - oilrig
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  selection_parent:
    ParentImage|endswith: '\w3wp.exe'
  selection_child:
    Image|endswith:
      - '\cmd.exe'
      - '\powershell.exe'
      - '\pwsh.exe'
      - '\cscript.exe'
      - '\wscript.exe'
      - '\mshta.exe'
      - '\rundll32.exe'
      - '\regsvr32.exe'
      - '\certutil.exe'
      - '\bitsadmin.exe'
      - '\net.exe'
      - '\net1.exe'
      - '\whoami.exe'
      - '\nltest.exe'
      - '\systeminfo.exe'
      - '\quser.exe'
  filter_appcmd:
    ParentCommandLine|contains: 'appcmd'
  condition: selection_parent and selection_child and not filter_appcmd
falsepositives:
  - Custom IIS administration scripts (rare and should be allowlisted)
  - Exchange health monitoring (HealthMailbox / MSExchangeHM)
level: high
ucs.rationale: OilRig's intrusion playbook against Middle-East telco, government, and energy targets has consistently centred on web-shelling internet-facing IIS / Exchange / OWA servers — TwoFace (Mandiant, 2017), RGDoor (Palo Alto Unit42, 2018), Karkoff/DNSpionage Exchange variants (Cisco Talos, 2019). The actor's tradecraft requires the web shell to execute commands via the IIS worker, producing a parent of w

_field_issues.message: `IsInitiatingProcessRemoteSession` is not a canonical column of DeviceProcessEvents. Did you mean `InitiatingProcessTokenElevation`?

_sigma_issues: sigma parse error: Sigma tag must start with namespace separated with dot from remaining tag.
